whatItDoes: Looks up the manufacturer or vendor associated with a MAC address by matching its first 3 bytes (OUI prefix) against a built-in database of over 200 common vendors. Supports partial matching when no exact OUI is found.
whoShouldUse: Network administrators identifying unknown devices on their network, security analysts investigating MAC addresses in logs, and IT support staff tracking hardware inventory.
whyItExists: Identifying device manufacturers from MAC addresses helps with network inventory, security auditing, and troubleshooting. This tool provides instant vendor lookup without requiring an external API call.
useCases: Identify the manufacturer of an unknown device connected to your network
Verify that a MAC address matches the expected hardware vendor for a device
Investigate suspicious MAC addresses in firewall or DHCP logs
Track hardware vendors across your infrastructure for procurement and support
instructions: Enter a MAC address in any common format (colon-separated, dash-separated, or dot-separated)
Press Lookup to search the built-in OUI database
View the vendor/manufacturer associated with the MAC prefix
Click the result to copy the vendor name to your clipboard
examples: Input: 'DC:A6:32:11:22:33' — Output: Vendor: Raspberry Pi Foundation
bestPractices: For exact vendor identification, verify the full OUI (first 6 hex characters) matches, not just the first 4
Keep in mind that MAC address spoofing is possible — the vendor tells you what hardware was manufactured, not necessarily what is currently running
Use partial matches as a starting point, then cross-reference with device serial numbers for confirmation
commonMistakes: Entering a full MAC address when only the OUI prefix (first 3 bytes) is needed for vendor lookup
Expecting 100% coverage — some OUI entries may not be in the built-in database
Confusing locally administered MAC addresses (second-least-significant bit of first byte set) with globally unique ones
faq: Why is my MAC not found? — The built-in database covers 200+ common vendors. For a complete lookup, try a service like IEEE's OUI registry.
What is an OUI? — Organizationally Unique Identifier, the first 24 bits of a MAC address assigned to hardware manufacturers by IEEE.
Can a MAC address be spoofed? — Yes, software can change the reported MAC address, so the vendor lookup shows the manufacturer of the original hardware, not necessarily the current configuration.
